questions.question: What is meant by a power of a number?
questions.correct_answer: A power represents repeated multiplication of the same number

questions.question: What is the base in an exponential expression?
questions.correct_answer: The base is the number that is multiplied repeatedly
questions.explanation: In 3^4, 3 is the base and 4 is the exponent.

questions.question: What is the exponent in a power?
questions.correct_answer: The exponent tells how many times the base is multiplied

questions.question: What is a square number?
questions.correct_answer: A number obtained by multiplying a number by itself

questions.question: What is a cube number?
questions.correct_answer: A number obtained by multiplying a number three times by itself

questions.question: What happens when powers with the same base are multiplied?
What happens when powers with the same base are divided?
What is the value of any number raised to the power 1?
What is the value of any non-zero number raised to the power 0?
How are powers of 10 useful?
questions.correct_answer: They help represent very large numbers easily

notes.title: Exponents
notes.content: Exponents represent repeated multiplication of a number by itself.

notes.title: Square Numbers
notes.content: A square number is the result of multiplying a number by itself.

notes.title: Cube Numbers
notes.content: A cube number is the result of multiplying a number three times by itself.

notes.title: Laws of Exponents
notes.content: When multiplying powers with the same base, add exponents. When dividing, subtract exponents.

notes.title: Powers of 10
notes.content: Powers of 10 help simplify the writing and understanding of large numbers.

facts.fact: 1 raised to any power is always 1.
The square of a number always produces a perfect square.
Cube numbers represent volumes in geometry.
Powers of 10 increase by adding one zero each time the exponent increases by 1.
